title: Use scheduled atomic checks instead of illegal infinite loops for GUI auto-reply tasks
content: For GUI automation tasks that must monitor the screen continuously, replace an in-process infinite loop with a scheduler-driven atomic action that runs once per invocation. A reusable pattern is: perform one screenshot-based check, detect whether a target contact has sent a new message, send a predefined typed reply if matched, then exit; have a task scheduler trigger this action periodically (for example, every 15 seconds) with immediate start and indefinite repetition. This avoids blocking execution and is more robust than relying on a sandboxed dead loop.
